label: ASD Essential Eight
description: Eight prioritized mitigation strategies from the Australian Signals Directorate to protect against the most common cyber threats. Organized into three maturity levels (ML1–ML3).

ML1.label: Maturity Level One
ML1.description: Partly aligned with the intent of the mitigation strategy

ML2.label: Maturity Level Two
ML2.description: Mostly aligned with the intent of the mitigation strategy

ML3.label: Maturity Level Three
ML3.description: Fully aligned with the intent of the mitigation strategy

P1.label: Application Control
P1.description: Execution of unapproved programs is prevented on workstations and servers

P2.label: Patch Applications
P2.description: Security vulnerabilities in applications are patched or mitigated within an appropriate timeframe

P3.label: Configure Microsoft Office Macro Settings
P3.description: Microsoft Office macros are disabled for users that do not have a demonstrated business requirement

P4.label: User Application Hardening
P4.description: Web browsers and applications are hardened to reduce the attack surface

P5.label: Restrict Administrative Privileges
P5.description: Requests for privileged access are validated and privileged accounts are restricted and monitored

P6.label: Patch Operating Systems
P6.description: Security vulnerabilities in operating systems are patched or mitigated within an appropriate timeframe

P7.label: Multi-Factor Authentication
P7.description: Stronger authentication is required to access sensitive data and systems

P8.label: Regular Backups
P8.description: Data, applications, and configuration settings are backed up and can be restored

m365Coverage.note: Essential Eight coverage through M365 configuration assessment focuses on strategies assessable via cloud settings. P8 (Regular Backups) is not mapped because backup validation requires infrastructure-level assessment beyond M365 configuration export.
